episodes.title: ‘Classic manipulative behaviour’: Disturbing emails show Jeffrey Epstein’s medical abuse
episodes.description:
‘Kinsey Schofield Unfiltered’ host Kinsey Schofield reacts to new emails revealing “calculated” Jeffrey Epstein withheld medical treatments and used his own doctors to exercise control over the women he was involved with. 

“It was not just this physical abuse, it wasn’t just about money or access, he literally used doctors in medical care to control women, deciding who got treatment and when,” Ms Schofield told Sky News host Rita Panahi. 

“That is classic manipulative behaviour, rewarding compliance, isolating people, keeping them dependent.
“That’s why these new emails are so disturbing.

“They show how much thought went into keeping these women under his control.”

episodes.title: Princess Beatrice and Eugenie banned from attending Royal Ascot due to Epstein links
episodes.description:
Sky News host Rita Panahi reveals reports Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie have been “banned” from attending Royal Ascot in June. 

“They won’t be allowed to join their family in the royal procession either,” Ms Panahi said.

“This is all due to the Epstein revelations surrounding their parents.”

episodes.title: Pressure mounts for Trump deposition after Clinton’s day-long Epstein testimony
episodes.description:
Former US President Bill Clinton issued a video statement after testifying before a US House committee regarding his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. 

Clinton maintained he had no knowledge of Epstein's crimes, stating, "I saw nothing, and I did nothing wrong." 

The testimony, which lasted nearly a full day, followed Hillary Clinton's deposition the previous day. 

Clinton's statement emphasised that no person is above the law, even presidents. 

The House Oversight Committee continues to push for President Donald Trump's testimony due to his past association with Epstein.

episodes.title: '50 shades of Fergie’: Sarah Ferguson mocked for Hollywood push amid Epstein files fallout
episodes.description:
‘Kinsey Schofield Unfiltered’ host Kinsey Schofield mocks Sarah Ferguson for desperately attempting to break into Hollywood before the arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor. 

“Timing is everything in Hollywood, and Fergie trying to sell her corset-ripping story as the Epstein files were dropped was the definition of bad timing,” Ms Schofield told Sky News host Rita Panahi.

“No one wants to associate Sarah with sexy time in the middle of one of the biggest global sex scandals in history. 

“50 shades of Fergie, I need to bleach my brain. No thank you.”

episodes.title: ‘Shocking emails’ reveal Epstein allegedly blamed Charles for Andrew’s trade envoy job loss
episodes.description:
Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power talks about the “extraordinary” news revealed about Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, Jeffrey Epstein and King Charles. 

“Shocking emails show that Jeffrey Epstein allegedly blamed King Charles when Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor lost his job as UK trade envoy,” Ms Power said. 

“Andrew became the UK special representative for international trade and investment in 2001, but resigned in 2011 over his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

“On July 21, 2011, which was the day that Andrew’s resignation was made public, Epstein allegedly sent an email to one of his associates saying ‘I assume he knows this is Charles’ doing’.”

episodes.title: Andrew’s personal protection team reveal what it’s like working for the disgraced former prince
episodes.description:
Broadcaster Esther Krakue suggests Andrew Mounbatten-Windsor was not “thought of very highly” when he was acting as the UK trade envoy. 

“Now that all these sorts of allegations have come up and the former prince Andrew has been arrested, you also hear individuals that were actually part of his personal protection … coming out saying that members of his protection team didn’t have a high opinion of him,” Ms Krakue told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.

“His relationship with Jeffrey Epstein clearly was more important to him than actually doing his role in serving the country.”

episodes.title: Epstein email blames King Charles for removing Andrew as UK trade envoy
episodes.description:
Emails discovered in the Epstein files appear to show Jeffrey Epstein blaming King Charles for Andrew’s removal as UK trade envoy.

The email exchange date is from July 21, 2011, the day after ex-prince Andrew was removed from the position.
 
Mr Epstein wrote, “I assume he knows that this is Charles doing.”

episodes.title: Bill Gates apologies over his links with Jeffrey Epstein
episodes.description:
Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates has apologised for his links with Jeffrey Epstein.

The 70-year-old billionaire told staffers at his foundation that he had flown on a private plane with Epstein and spent time with him abroad.

Mr Gates has denied any illegal wrongdoing.

episodes.title: Sarah Ferguson ‘handed over’ 22-year-old goddaughter to Epstein post-jail
episodes.description:
UK Barrister Andrew Eborn reacts to the “harrowing” revelation that Sarah Ferguson introduced her 22-year-old goddaughter to Jeffrey Epstein just one year after his release from jail.

“This comes again on the tsunami of releases about these three million documents, and I can tell you there’s going to be more and more coming out,” Mr Eborn told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.

“They expose Sarah Ferguson’s jaw-dropping betrayal.

“To introduce your own goddaughter to a person, at that stage, who was a convicted pedophile, Jeffrey Epstein, just a year after his release, is beggaring belief. 

“She was 22 years old, she was then, Poppy Cotterell and she was handed basically straight into Epstein’s orbit by the duchess of York herself.”

episodes.title: Fergie goes into hiding over explosive Epstein revelations as more secrets expected to be exposed
episodes.description:
Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth claims Sarah Ferguson appears to have “gone into hiding” following the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files.

“She looks terrible out of all of this as well, but she seems to have gone into hiding; she hasn’t been seen in public since December,” she told Sky News host Peta Credlin.

“She’s nowhere to be seen.

“More will certainly come out about her, I suspect.”

episodes.title: More trouble for Andrew as secret files on the former prince to be released
episodes.description:
Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth has explained what’s expected to be revealed in documents relating to Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s appointment as British trade envoy.

The UK government will release files on the former Duke of York and on Peter Mandelson’s role as the UK’s ambassador to the US after MPs backed the move.

“Now, there’s reporting here in the UK that Lord Mandelson was one of those who was pushing for Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor to be put into that role, so this is a spider’s web within parliament,” Ms Elsworth told Sky News Australia.

Ms Elsworth suspects this will put an “enormous amount” of pressure on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer due to his decision to appoint Mr Mandelson as the US ambassador despite knowledge of his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein.

episodes.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla put up united front amid Andrew-Epstein turmoil
episodes.description:
King Charles and Queen Camilla have put on a united front at an awards ceremony in London.

The Royals attended ‘The Queen Elizabeth Prizes for Higher and Further Education’ at St James’s Palace.

Around 19 universities and colleges were recognised for their innovation and excellence.

This comes amid turmoil over Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

The former Duke of York was arrested earlier last week but has been released and is now under investigation on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

episodes.title: ‘Passing state secrets’: UK Epstein cronies under fire for abusing government positions
episodes.description:
Former White House press secretary Sean Spicer claims Epstein cronies in the UK are under fire for allegedly passing state secrets, differing from those who stand accused in the US.

“It comes down to passing state secrets in their position of trust within the British government,” Mr Spicer told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.

“You don’t have that same accusation over here in the United States.

“There’s a big difference between being a scumbag – and hanging around with scumbags – and breaking the law.”

episodes.title: ‘Unseemliness’: Epstein files having global effect as Mandelson and Andrew fall
episodes.description:
Former White House Press secretary Sean Spicer details the fallout from the Epstein files and the key differences between how the United States and Great Britain are handling the scandal. 

“Some individuals, Larry Summers, one of the Pritzkers, a prominent family in America, resign from their position, there’s a casting agency, a talent agency out in Hollywood where the gentlemen is stepping aside, there was a chair of a very prominent law firm called Paul Weiss, he’s stepping down,” Mr Spicer told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.

“Look at what the Brits are going after them on, it’s not going after them on the relationship with Epstein per se with respect to the women … but I will say … it comes down to passing state secrets in their position of trust in the British government … what you have is unseemliness.”

episodes.title: Epstein scandal causing a ‘crisis’ for the British establishment
episodes.description:
Monash and Warwick University PHD Candidate Tom Howe says the Epstein scandal is causing a “crisis” for the British establishment.

Mr Howe told Sky News Australia it does “undermine” the British establishment as a whole.

Although Mr Howe claims it is "a gift to insurgent parties on the right and left".

episodes.title: UK locals express their ‘anger and resentment’ at Andrew’s Epstein ties as public turns on him
episodes.description:
Sky News host Chris Kenny reacts to some of the local reactions in the UK to Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s arrest and ties to Jeffrey Epstein, in which they expressed their “anger and resentment” at the former Duke of York.

“I think those people are talking a lot of sense,” he said.

Mr Kenny discussed the local reactions to the fallout over Mr Mountbatten-Windsor’s ties to Epstein with Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth.

episodes.title: Andrew braces for another ‘very grim week’ as Epstein scandal continues to worsen
episodes.description:
Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth speculates that it’s going to be another “grim week” for Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

Mr Mountbatten-Windsor continues to cop backlash as more revelations surface from the Jeffrey Epstein files.

The former Duke of York was also arrested earlier last week but later released under investigation on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

Mr Mountbatten-Windsor has denied any wrongdoing.

“I can assure you that it’s going to be another very grim week for Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor as the spotlight continues on him for his relationship and friendship that he had with Jeffrey Epstein,” Ms Elsworth told Sky News host Chris Kenny.

episodes.title: Fergie flees to the Middle East in search of wealth where she still ‘carries respect’
episodes.description:
Royal author Andrew Lownie claims Sarah Ferguson is busy in the Middle East trying to generate wealth amid the unfolding Epstein crisis.

“She’s in the Middle East, it’s the one place where she still carries some sort of respect,” Mr Lownie told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.

“She’s busy trying to get money off various rulers there.”

episodes.title: ‘She’s worth investigating’: Epstein files reveal Fergie’s ‘appalling’ misconduct
episodes.description:
Royal author Andrew Lownie says Sarah Ferguson is worth investigating over her ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

“Sarah Ferguson is a material witness to the sex trafficking, to what was going on in Epstein’s home,” Mr Lownie told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.

“She’s definitely worth an investigation as well, and she should certainly be questioned about the misconduct in public office.”

Ms Ferguson has yet to respond to the allegations from the Epstein files.

episodes.title: ‘More accountability’: Andrew-Epstein crisis sparks calls for King Charles’ abdication
episodes.description:
Royal author Andrew Lownie claims there are increasing calls for King Charles to abdicate the Crown to Prince William amid the Andrew-Epstein crisis.

“It’s now being shown that the King was warned about his brother six years ago and did nothing,” Mr Lownie told Sky News host Andrew Bolt.

“There are increasing calls that Charles has been compromised by this scandal, that the only way out is for him to abdicate.

“To pass it on to William, who, so far, has been seen to be completely clean.”

episodes.title: Andrew hit with damning new Epstein claims as saga continues to spiral out of control
episodes.description:
Former UK attorney general Sir Michael Ellis discusses former UK prime minister Gordon Brown’s claim that Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor may have used taxpayer-funded jets and RAF bases to meet with Jeffrey Epstein.

“I think we need to be very careful at the moment because obviously we have to bear in mind that the presumption of innocence applies,” he told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.

This comes after Mr Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested but later released under investigation on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

The former Duke of York has denied any wrongdoing in relation to Epstein.

episodes.title: ‘He knew’: King dragged into brother’s Epstein drama following new bombshell revelations
episodes.description:
GB News host Alex Armstrong discusses the continued fallout over Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s arrest earlier last week, with the King now dragged into the scandal.

“The King is now getting embroiled in this too, he’s on the front page of the Daily Mail with accusations that he too knew about Andrew’s what they’re calling ‘dodgy activities’ all the way back in 2019,” he told Sky News host James Macpherson.

“So, this is just blowing up here every single day.”

episodes.title: UK government not ruling out a judge-led inquiry into Andrew’s Epstein ties
episodes.description:
The UK government is not ruling out a judge-led inquiry into Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s ties to convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.

Authorities are conducting searches at the former Prince of York’s old home in Windsor after he was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in a public office.

Mr Mountbatten-Windsor has denied any wrongdoing.

episodes.title: Epstein-linked Andrew remains under investigation following humiliating arrest
episodes.description:
Disgraced ex-Prince Andrew will remain under investigation following his arrest on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

On Thursday, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was arrested on his 66th birthday, marking the first time in modern history a Royal Family member was taken into custody.

The disgraced prince was arrested under suspicion that he shared sensitive information with paedophile Jeffrey Epstein when serving as a UK trade envoy.

Following his arrest, UK police were seen searching Andrew’s Windsor Estate and the Wood Farm property, where he served as an envoy from 2001 to 2011.

The ex-prince has strongly denied any wrongdoing in connection with sex trafficker and paedophile, Epstein.

episodes.title: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor released from police custody after arrest
episodes.description:
Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor has been released from police custody in Norfolk after his arrest.

The former prince was arrested at his home on Sandringham Estate in Norfolk on his 66th birthday.

Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor has always denied any wrongdoing regarding his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

episodes.title: Former prince Andrew could face ‘maximum sentence of life in prison’
episodes.description:
Talk TV host Samara Gill reveals that there has not currently been any statement from the royal family after the arrest of former prince Andrew. 

“It was unmarked officers that went into his, almost holding pen that he’s in at the moment,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Caleb Bond.

“They [the police] went in there, and they were seen taking laptops out and they’ve basically taken him.

“If he’s proven guilty, then there’s a maximum sentence of life imprisonment for Andrew.”

episodes.title: ‘Now a proper criminal investigation’: Former prince Andrew arrested on his 66th birthday
episodes.description:
ITV News Royal Editor Chris Ship details the alleged arrest of former prince Andrew.

“Confirmation from one of our police forces, it’s Thames Valley Police, which is the police force which looks after Windsor, they’ve confirmed this morning that they have made an arrest of a man in his 60’s,” Mr Ship told Sky News host Paul Murray.

“Guess whose birthday it is today? Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor is 66 years old today.

“He’s been arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

“We have to be super careful when it comes to an active police investigation, and a man, the King's brother, is under arrest.

“It is now a proper criminal investigation.”

episodes.title: BREAKING: Shocking arrest of former prince Andrew
episodes.description:
Reports have emerged the former Prince Andrew has been arrested by police at his home near the Sandringham Estate. 

The BBC has reported eight officers arrived in six Metropolitan Police vehicles and have taken Andrew into custody. 

The arrest is allegedly related to potential misconduct in public office during his time as a UK trade envoy, with suggestions that he may have improperly shared privileged information, potentially with Jeffrey Epstein.

While the details are still unfolding, this development marks a significant turn in the ongoing scrutiny surrounding Andrew's ties to Epstein and the accusations against him.

episodes.title: Former Victoria’s Secret CEO Les Wexner questioned over Epstein links
episodes.description:
The billionaire founder of Victoria's Secret Les Wexner has been questioned by members of the US House Oversight Committee over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

Les Wexner's finances were handled by Mr Epstein, and despite never being charged, Mr Wexner has been named as a co-conspirator in recently revealed documents.

Mr Wexner denies any wrongdoing and says he ‘was not a participant’ in Jeffrey Epstein’s crimes.

episodes.title: 'So cringy’: Whoopi Goldberg exposed in Epstein files after alleged ‘no thanks’ reply
episodes.description:
Writer and comedian Michael Loftus ridicules ‘The View’ co-host Whoopi Goldberg, who has denied links to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein after her name appeared in the recently released files.

“This is so cringy … she wanted to be close with Jeffrey Epstein, she wanted in,” Mr Loftus told Sky News host Rita Panahi.

“She doesn’t say his response, he just wrote back two words, no thanks.

“Epstein turned her down; she's going to be going to therapy for years to get over that.”

episodes.title: Whoopi Goldberg on the Epstein files after demanding immediate release
episodes.description:
Sky News host Chris Kenny says Whoopi Goldberg is on the Epstein files after she demanded the release of the documents. 

“While we're on leftist media, let me show you something quite delightful,” Mr Kenny said.

“Remember when the loony lefties of The View, that’s that American show which is a TV version of the Teals. Well, when they thought the Epstein Files would do in Donald Trump, they just wanted everything revealed immediately.

“Fast forward a little while, and guess who's turned up in the files, none other than Goldberg herself.”

episodes.title: Hillary Clinton labelled ‘psychopath’ after ‘lying’ about her relationship with Jeffrey Epstein
episodes.description:
Comedian Alex Stein reacts to former US first lady Hillary Clinton claiming she had no links to Jeffrey Epstein. 

“Hillary Clinton is a psychopath,” Mr Stein told Sky News host Rita Panahi. 

“She has the ability to lie better than any Hollywood actress.”

episodes.title: Hillary Clinton lies about ties to Epstein despite infamous Bill Clinton portrait
episodes.description:
Sky News host Rita Panahi slams former US first lady Hillary Clinton for her “easy ability to lie with complete confidence” about her and her husband, former US president Bill Clinton’s “close relationship” with Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell. 

Ms Panahi also comments on Bill Clinton’s “bizarre relationship” with Jeffrey Epstein, since Mr Epstein hung a huge painting of Mr Clinton in his New York residence wearing the “infamous blue dress” Monica Lewinsky was rumoured to be wearing during her encounter with the former US president.

“Here she explains to the BBC that her and her husband Bill Clinton had no relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell,” Ms Panahi said. 

“Never mind the fact that Bill Clinton was a regular on the Lolita Express. 

“Hillary Clinton now claims there was no relationship, no links with Epstein and Maxwell and the Clintons.”

episodes.title: Allegations suggest Sarah Ferguson embarrassingly begged Epstein for ‘house assistant’ job
episodes.description:
Newly revealed emails tied to Jeffrey Epstein allege that Sarah Ferguson begged the paedophile for a job as she “desperately” needed money.

The email that exposed Ferguson’s latest embarrassing moment with the paedophile follows the latest release of files tied to Epstein.

“But why I don't understand, don't you just get me to be your House Assistant,” Ferguson’s alleged email read.

“I am the most capable and desperately need the money.

"Please Jeffrey think about it.”

Following the alleged email sent by Ferguson resurfacing, royal experts lambasted the ex-wife of disgraced Prince Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

Hillary Fordwich blasted Ferguson for “begging” the paedophile for money, saying, “This is beyond the pale”.

episodes.title: 'The View' co-host Whoopi Goldberg addresses links to paedophile Jeffrey Epstein
episodes.description:
‘The View’ co-host Whoopi Goldberg has shut down links to paedophile Jeffrey Epstein after her name appeared in the recently released files.

On Tuesday, Goldberg revealed her name appeared in the files and claimed she was addressing it “in the name of transparency”.

Goldberg's name was mentioned in a 2013 email, where an individual says she needs a plane to Monaco and that “John Lennon's charity is paying for it”.

The actress claimed that the email should have said Julian Lennon’s charity, adding that it went on to ask Epstein if he would offer his plane to Goldberg.

Fellow co-host Joy Behar claimed that “anybody can be on this list”, before Goldberg defended herself for appearing in the files.

Well, this is my point. Because I’m telling you, when I tell you people are trying to turn me into, I wasn’t his girlfriend. I wasn’t his friend,” Goldberg said.

episodes.title: Hillary Clinton makes wild accusation against Trump admin ahead of Epstein deposition
episodes.description:
Hillary Clinton is accusing the Trump administration of a cover-up over its handling of the Epstein files.

Ms Clinton is due to testify before a Congressional committee on the Epstein files on February 26, while her husband, former US president Bill Clinton, is set to appear on February 27.

Ms Clinton says they have both been cooperating with the investigation, but their order to appear in person is a distraction.

“I just want it to be fair; I want everybody treated the same way. That’s not true for my husband and me because other witnesses were asked to testify, they gave written statements under oath,” she said.

“We offered that. They want to pull us – why do they want to pull us into this? To divert attention from President Trump.”

episodes.title: Andrew could face prison time over new shocking Epstein allegations
episodes.description:
News Corp Columnist Angela Mollard weighs in on new allegations surrounding Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and his ties to Jeffrey Epstein. 

Newly released Epstein files accuse the former prince of forwarding government documents to the convicted paedophile. 

“What we’re seeing now is allegations, coming through the Epstein files, that he was actually sharing this privileged information around itineraries, business deals with Epstein, things that could have had an impact on national security,” Ms Mollard told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.

“The implications of this, of course, is that if he is charged for misconduct in public office, that could potentially come with a prison sentence, so huge.

“How the Palace handles this, there’s still a lot to run on this story.”

episodes.title: Pam Bondi releases shocking Epstein list of ‘prominent names’
episodes.description:
‘Kinsey Schofield Unfiltered’ host Kinsey Schofield weighs in on Attorney General Pam Bondi releasing a list of “prominent names” in the Epstein files. 

“There are a lot of names on this list, and they are people that in no way, shape or form had anything to do with Jeffrey Epstein, so I would beg people to keep that in mind before judging someone because their name is on the list,” Ms Schofield told Sky News host Rita Panahi.

“The real question investigators look for is evidence of criminal participation, not just association.

“Yes, prominent names do appear on this list, but what matters legally is whether there’s proof tied to actual offences.”

episodes.title: Andrew torn into for allegedly making palace staff use ‘sick code names’ to let in young women
episodes.description:
TalkTV host Samara Gill has reacted to a report on a disturbing code word allegedly used by Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor for palace staffers to let young women in for visits. 

“It’s irreprehensible behaviour from Andrew and he was able to get away with it for years,” she told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio. 

“I just feel sorry for those poor staff having to use those sick code names of ‘Mrs Windsor’.”

episodes.title: Over 300 high-profile names shockingly listed in latest Jeffrey Epstein file release
episodes.description:
US Attorney General Pam Bondi has released a list of over 300 high-profile individuals mentioned in the Jeffrey Epstein files.

In a letter penned by Bondi and Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, they announced “all” of the Epstein files have been released.

Bondi and Blanche did note that privileged material is still withheld due to government officials and “politically exposed” individuals appearing in the files.

“In accordance with the requirements of the Act, and as described in various Department submissions to the courts of the Southern District of New York assigned to the Epstein and Maxwell prosecutions and related orders, the Department released all ‘records, documents, communications and investigative materials in the possession of the Department’ that ‘relate to’ any of nine different categories,” Bondi and Blanche wrote.

Included in the list are names previously reported, such as US President Donald Trump, Bill Clinton, Bill Gates and Woody Allen.

Other names not previously covered but that are listed include Barack and Michelle Obama, Kurt Cobain, Prince Harry, Robert De Niro and Bruce Springsteen.

The inclusion of these individuals in the files does not imply any wrongdoing or direct contact with Epstein.

episodes.title: ‘Sickly’ Epstein emails show Sarah Ferguson put daughters ‘almost in harm’s way’
episodes.description:
TalkTV host Samara Gill claims Sarah Ferguson’s emails to Jeffrey Epstein are “sickly”. 

“Her emails are sickly towards Epstein,” she told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio. 

“She loved the lavish lifestyle that he provided so much so that yes, I would say she put her daughters ... almost in harm’s way and met up with him after he was convicted of paedophile offences.”

episodes.title: ‘Sleazy’ Andrew’s ‘creepy code word’ for palace staff to let young girls in exposed
episodes.description:
Sky News host Danica De Giorgio discusses a “creepy code word” Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor allegedly used when “ordering palace staff to let a string of young women in for visits”. 

“This is really shocking. He allegedly used a word – this is according to The Sun,” she said. 

“It says emails released as part of the Epstein files show several women entered the palace with the former prince’s permission after being supplied by his paedo pal. 

“Sources told The Sun a string of young women were invited to the palace by Andrew, and he made a specific passphrase. 

“This paraphrase Andrew is thought to have said to them: ‘Mrs Windsor will arrive shortly, please let her in and show her up’.” 

Ms De Giorgio said surely Palace staffers would have seen Andrew’s “sleazy behaviour”.

episodes.title: ‘I just really thought that I was going to die’: Epstein survivor details distressing experience
episodes.description:
A woman who was trafficked from Cape Town to Jeffrey Epstein’s private island has revealed details of the sexual abuse she suffered.

Juliette Bryant says she feared for her life, describing how she was trapped in the “invisible chains”.

“I was really, really petrified, and it really changed me, you know, the whole thing going through this,” she said.

“I just really thought that I was going to die.”

episodes.title: ‘Not the end of the story’: Many claim Epstein files still incomplete
episodes.description:
Former White House chief of staff Mick Mulvaney claims he was “surprised” after Attorney General Pam Bondi announced all the Epstein files were released.

“I’m not sure that’s the end of the story,” Mr Mulvaney told Sky News Australia.

“I don’t see this going away.”

episodes.title: Full Epstein files release sees ‘heads roll’ and causes ‘ripple effect’ across the world
episodes.description:
TalkTV host Samara Gill has praised US President Donald Trump for committing to his election promise to release the Jeffrey Epstein files.

US Attorney General Pam Bondi has released all of the Epstein files, including several redactions and a list of high-profile names appearing in the documents.

“My God, what a ripple effect it has had on the world. We’ve seen heads rolling from Westminster to Florida to Washington to all over the world,” Ms Gill told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.

“I just really do think that we should commend Donald Trump for actually releasing them and Pam Bondi for doing her bit and putting the pressure on.”

episodes.title: Peter Mandelson asked to testify before US Congress over Epstein links
episodes.description:
The former UK ambassador to the US Peter Mandelson has been seen in public for the first time since he was asked to testify in front of the US Congress over his links to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

He has been given a deadline of February 27 to respond to Congress request for evidence.

This comes as the UK Metropolitan Police is investigating Mr Mandelson on suspicion of misconduct in public office following revelations from the latest release of documents from the Epstein files.

episodes.title: Peter Mandelson asked to give evidence into Jeffrey Epstein links
episodes.description:
Democrats have written to Peter Mandelson asking him to give evidence in the ongoing Jeffrey Epstein investigation.

The Democrats of the Oversight Committee, the congressional committee on Capitol Hill investigating crimes involving Jeffrey Epstein, have called for those who have been mentioned in the files to come forward to the committee and tell them what they know.

Mr Mandelson has not responded at this stage, but they believe he has questions to answer on Capitol Hill.

episodes.title: Epstein emails lifts lid on Ghislaine Maxwell’s ‘strong ties’ to the Clintons
episodes.description:
Sky News host Rita Panahi analyses more emails from the Epstein files which shine a new light on the Clintons.

“Now more on those emails in the Epstein files, which have also lifted the lid on Ghislaine Maxwell’s strong ties to the Clinton Global Initiative, one of Bill Clinton’s post-presidential projects,” she said.

“Maxwell reportedly played a major role in funding and establishing the Clintons’ charity.”

episodes.title: Andrew’s ‘huge abuse of his position’ after leaking confidential information to Epstein
episodes.description:
GB News host Emily Carver claims the public wants to see “true accountability” from Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor following damning allegations in the Epstein files.

The Royal Family has been under scrutiny with King Charles heckled twice during his walkabouts over his brother’s friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and recent revelations he had been leaking confidential information to the convicted paedophile.

There’s no suggestion of any wrongdoing, and Mr Mountbatten-Windsor has always denied allegations against him.

“The allegations are that he may have abused his role (as trade envoy) by sharing confidential reports with Jeffrey Epstein, which would be a huge abuse of his position,” Ms Carver told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.

episodes.title: ‘This is the smoking gun’: Andrew’s latest ‘dodgy’ move exposed
episodes.description:
SkyNews.com.au columnist Louise Roberts says there is a lot of “anger” towards Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor as the public finds out he potentially traded top-secret information with Jeffrey Epstein when he was the trade envoy. 

“This is the smoking gun for Andrew,” Ms Roberts told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power. 

“The issue now is whether his official privilege extended to giving information to a convicted sex offender.

“He has to answer for it because it's one thing to have dodgy friendships … he’s never really been held accountable for anything around that trade envoy role.

“This is going to be a moment of reckoning for him.”

episodes.title: Epstein accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell cowardly pleads the Fifth Amendment during deposition
episodes.description:
Jeffrey Epstein's accomplice, Ghislaine Maxwell, pleaded the Fifth Amendment during the House Oversight Committee's deposition.

On Monday, Maxwell appeared for a closed-door interview in a bipartisan probe by the House into the federal government’s handling of the Epstein files.

Before the deposition began, Maxwell’s lawyer asserted that Maxwell could not implicate US President Donald Trump and Bill Clinton of any wrongdoing.

According to a statement by Maxwell’s attorney, David Oscar Markus, the sex trafficker will answer questions but only if President Trump reduces her sentence.

“If this Committee and the American public truly want to hear the unfiltered truth about what happened, there is a straightforward path. Ms Maxwell is prepared to speak fully and honestly if granted clemency by President Trump,” Markus said.

episodes.title: ‘Phew, you still love me’: ‘Degenerate’ Fergie’s ‘desperate’ emails to Epstein exposed
episodes.description:
Sky News host Rita Panahi claims emails revealed in the latest instalment of the Epstein files reveal Sarah Ferguson was “regularly chasing money and attention” from the convicted paedophile.

“Former Duchess of York Sarah Ferguson has been labelled ‘desperate and a degenerate’ in the latest instalment of the Epstein files, with emails between her and Jeffrey Epstein revealing she was regularly chasing money and attention from the convicted paedophile,” she said.

“The exchanges seemingly indicate that the disgraced ex-wife of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was very attached to Jeffrey Epstein, telling him he was her ‘pillar’.

“There’s no coming back from this.”

There’s no suggestion of any wrongdoing, and Sarah Ferguson has yet to respond to any of the allegations from the Epstein files.

episodes.title: US congressman warns Epstein revelations could spell ‘the end’ of the British monarchy
episodes.description:
Democrat Congressman Ro Khanna has warned that the fallout from the Jeffrey Epstein scandal could threaten the survival of the British monarchy.

“This is the most vulnerable the British monarchy has ever been… maybe this will be the end of the monarchy,” Khanna said.

He also singled out Peter Mandelson, arguing he could “bring down” the entire UK government.

Khanna called on King Charles to answer for what he knew about Prince Andrew and that stripping his title is “not enough.”

This comes as UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces calls to resign over Mandelson’s close ties to Epstein.

episodes.title: Epstein’s ‘pretty harsh’ comment about Sarah Ferguson
episodes.description:
Sky News host Rita Panahi has commented on a “pretty harsh” remark from Jeffrey Epstein about Sarah Ferguson.

“Epstein was not so complimentary about Fergie,” she said.

“In an email from September 2011 ... Epstein sends a link to the Daily Mail’s coverage of Princess Beatrice’s graduation from university, and he writes, ‘pic of F from Friday – F being Fergie – not the prettiest site’.

“Gosh, yeah, it’s pretty harsh.”

episodes.title: ‘Actually gut-churning’: Sarah Ferguson’s ‘sickening’ emails to Epstein about her daughters
episodes.description:
Broadcaster Dee Dee Dunleavy has reacted to some of the emails Sarah Ferguson sent to Jeffrey Epstein after he was convicted of being a paedophile.

“Two days after he was released from prison, his assistant approached him and asked him would he pay for Sarah and for the girls to go to Miami. Now, we don’t know that he did pay for them,” she told Sky News host Rita Panahi.

“Then we see other emails after that where he’s asking for his business contacts to be shown around Buckingham Palace by Sarah Ferguson, and would the girls be there; would the girls be able to show them around.

“It’s almost like she’s pimping them out, and it’s actually gut-churning; it’s sickening.”

There’s no suggestion of any wrongdoing, and Sarah Ferguson has yet to respond to any of the allegations from the Epstein files.

episodes.title: Shocking new emails reveal Andrew leaked ‘secret and confidential information’ to Epstein
episodes.description:
Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth claims new emails reveal Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor was allegedly giving “secret and confidential information” over to Jeffrey Epstein when he was the UK’s trade envoy.

“This is deeply concerning for everyone involved here in the UK,” she told Sky News Australia.

“Now, he has always, always denied any wrongdoing in relation to his connection with Jeffrey Epstein, but he hasn’t spoken publicly in recent times on these matters, and it’s unlikely that he will.”

episodes.title: King Charles breaks silence over explosive Andrew-Epstein allegations
episodes.description:
Buckingham Palace has released a statement over the allegations against Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor’s links to Jeffrey Epstein.

“The King has made clear, in words and through unprecedented actions, his profound concern at allegations which continue to come to light in respect of Mr Mountbatten-Windsor’s conduct,” the statement read.

“If we are approached by Thames Valley Police, we stand ready to support them.”

This comes after the Prince and Princess of Wales released a statement saying they were “deeply concerned” by the “continuing revelations” surrounding Jeffrey Epstein and his relationship with Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

episodes.title: Keir Starmer's chief of staff quits over Peter Mandelson appointment amid Epstein scandal
episodes.description:
UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is facing pressure to resign after ignoring evidence that Lord Peter Mandelson was closely associated with convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein.

Despite this, Mr Starmer appointed Mandelson as the US ambassador. The Epstein files allegedly show Mandelson providing Epstein with inside information. 

Instead of taking responsibility for hiring Mandelson, Starmer has effectively fired his chief of staff, Morgan McSweeney, who was reportedly influential in the decision to appoint Mandelson.

episodes.title: Epstein files allege ex-Prince Andrew shared confidential reports with disgraced financier
episodes.description:
‘Kinsey Schofield Unfiltered’ host Kinsey Schofield weighs in on recently released Epstein files showing former prince Andrew apparently passing on confidential reports to Jeffrey Epstein whilst he was the UK trade envoy.

“Everyone keeps saying, ' How did he [Jeffrey Epstein] make his money? By selling access at the highest level of politics, royalty, finance, and tech,” Ms Schofield told Sky News host Rita Panahi.

“For Andrew, official guidance says trade envoys have a duty of confidentiality with sensitive commercial or political information.

“He might really be in trouble here.”

episodes.title: Footage from Epstein files shows Ghislaine Maxwell denying giving massages
episodes.description:
Sky News host Rita Panahi blasts Ghislaine Maxwell for denying giving convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein a massage despite photographic evidence.

Footage from newly released Epstein files shows the sworn deposition of Ghislaine Maxwell repeatedly denying giving massages.

“We know that's not true, we’ve all seen her give the convicted paedophile a foot massage with her shirt undone,” Ms Panahi said.

episodes.title: Bizarre revelation in Epstein case as official death announcement dated one day before paedo died
episodes.description: A bizarre inconsistency has surfaced in the Jeffrey Epstein case after millions of new files were released.

episodes.title: ‘Untenable’: Explosive Epstein scandal could be the ‘final nails in Starmer’s coffin’
episodes.description:
Sky News host James Macpherson claims Keir Starmer’s leadership position “looks untenable” following revelations he appointed Peter Mandelson as the UK’s US ambassador despite knowing of his links to Jeffrey Epstein.

“I do note that Mandelson denies any wrongdoing,” he said.

“But a couple of things have happened over the past 24 hours to heap more pressure on Keir Starmer.”

episodes.title: Royal Family knew of ‘very bad’ emails between Andrew and Epstein
episodes.description:
deeply disgraced former prince Andrew was involved in his email exchanges with Jeffrey Epstein.

“I can tell you already, the Palace had known that there are very, very bad emails,” Ms Barak told Sky News host Danica De Giorgio.

“After I exposed Andrew’s lies, they stripped him from his duke title, and they knew how much damage was coming.”

episodes.title: King Charles ignores question about Jeffrey Epstein investigation
episodes.description:
King Charles chose not to react to a question put to him about the Jeffrey Epstein investigation whilst meeting the public today.

The King was on a visit to the Essex village of Dedham when a heckler shouted at him.

Last year, King Charles stripped his brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor of his royal titles.

episodes.title: ‘I am sorry’: Keir Starmer apologies to Jeffrey Epstein victims
episodes.description:
UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has apologised to the victims of Jeffrey Epstein for appointing Peter Mandelson as the country’s ambassador to the US.

Mr Starmer is battling for his political future as he struggles to maintain the scandal.

“I am sorry, sorry for what was done to you. Sorry that so many people with power failed you. Sorry for having believed Mandelson’s lies and appointed him,” Mr Starmer said.

episodes.title: ‘Unbelievable sadness’: Melinda Gates heartbreaking reaction to Bill Gates-Epstein allegations
episodes.description: Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power has reacted to Melinda Gates' heartbreaking reaction to shocking allegations against Bill Gates in the new Epstein files.

episodes.title: Second woman alleges she was sent to UK by Jeffrey Epstein for Andrew
episodes.description:
UK Barrister Andrew Eborn comments on reports a second woman claims she was brought to the UK by Jeffrey Epstein for the disgraced former prince Andrew.

“The amount of grace and favours and sort of benefits that Andrew used to give Epstein and the various people trafficked by Epstein … that’s what some of these three million documents have revealed is the extent of that access,” Mr Eborn told Sky News Digital Presenter Gabriella Power.

“There’s a second woman, as predicted, and there will be more revelations, I can guarantee, as people continue to comb through those three million documents. 

“She says she was sent to the UK by Jeffrey Epstein for a sexual encounter with the then prince Andrew … they put the pressure on the King, the King Charles lawyer, to issue a real apology.

“Questions need to be answered, who knew what, when, that needs to be looked at.”

episodes.title: ‘Damage is already done’: Bill Gates attempts to erase latest Epstein claim in new interview
episodes.description:
Writer Esther Krakue claims the “damage is already done” for Bill Gates after new allegations about him surfaced from the Epstein files. 

“The allegation that he presumably got an STD from a Russian prostitute that was associated with Jeffrey Epstein was actually written in a note by Jeffrey Epstein to himself,” Ms Krakue told Sky News host Chris Kenny. 

“So, it wasn’t actually a form of correspondence.

“They’re allegations that he wrote that for the purpose of explicit blackmailing.”

episodes.title: ‘The brother I have always wished for’: Sarah Ferguson’s emails to Epstein released
episodes.description:
The latest batch of documents released by the US Justice Department related to r Jeffrey Epstein includes emails and exchanges involving Prince Andrew and his ex-wife, Sarah Ferguson. 

The files reveal an email exchange between Epstein and Andrew about a potential dinner companion.

In the files was also a message from Sarah Ferguson thanking Epstein for his support and kindness.

episodes.title: Shocking exchange of Epstein and former Prince Andrew discussing a Russian woman
episodes.description:
The US Justice Department is releasing more than three million pages of documents related to the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

This includes more than 2 thousand videos and 180 thousand images and includes never-before-seen correspondence between the sex offender and the former Prince Andrew. 

One email exchange from August 2010 shows Epstein offering to introduce Andrew to a 'Russian' woman, to which Andrew replied positively. 

The emails also suggest Andrew was frustrated about not being able to participate in certain opportunities.

episodes.title: House panel votes to hold Clintons in contempt over Epstein probe
episodes.description:
The US House Oversight Committee has voted to hold former US president Bill Clinton and his wife Hillary in contempt of Congress.

The couple is refusing to meet the panel over its investigation into convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

Some Democrats predict that potentially prosecuting a former president will backfire.

episodes.title: Trump calls the Epstein Files a hoax
episodes.description:
US President Donald Trump is blaming the Democrats for the latest Epstein files release and is accusing them of pushing a hoax.

"Now one million more pages on Epstein are found. DOJ is being forced to spend all of its time on this Democrat inspired Hoax,” Mr Trump said.

According to the US government, the rest of the Epstein files will be released in the new year.

episodes.title: Evidence against Ghislaine Maxwell to be released amid Epstein file drama
episodes.description:
A federal judge in the US has ruled the Justice Department can publicly release hundreds of investigative documents from the sex trafficking case involving Ghislaine Maxwell.

The decision follows the passage of the Epstein Files Transparency Act, which was signed into law last month.

The ruling means the department can publicly release evidence gathered against Ghislaine Maxwell.

episodes.title: Andrew 'avoiding the US like a plague' to avoid being compelled to testify about Epstein
episodes.description:
‘Kinsey Schofield Unfiltered’ host Kinsey Schofield says “humiliating” Andrew Mountbatten Windsor will “avoid the United States like a plague”. 

“Pressure is mounting for him to testify before a key US congressional committee,” Ms Schofield told Sky News host Rita Panahi. 

“Members of the House Oversight Committee are urging him to disclose any information he may have about Jeffrey Epstein’s activities. 

“If Andrew travels to the United States, he would fall under congressional jurisdiction and could be compelled to testify.”

episodes.title: Younger generation of royals ‘thoroughly repulsed’ by Prince Andrew allegations
episodes.description:
Royal historian and writer Tessa Dunlop says younger generations of royals are feeling “thoroughly repulsed” by allegations against Prince Andrew.

Buckingham Palace released a statement stating Prince Andrew’s new name will be Andrew Mountbatten Windsor.

Ms Dunlop told Sky News Australia that it “took a lot” for King Charles to remove Prince Andrew’s royal titles, despite years of controversy and allegations against him.

episodes.title: ‘Regular old Andrew’: Buckingham Palace remove Prince Andrew’s royal titles
episodes.description:
Sky News contributor Sophie Elsworth discusses the removal of Prince Andrew’s royal titles.

Buckingham Palace released a statement stating Prince Andrew’s new name will be Andrew Mountbatten Windsor.

Ms Elsworth told Sky News Australia that he will now become “regular old Andrew”.

episodes.title: ‘Typical way of a cult’: Australians ‘sick’ of being forced to adopt net zero
episodes.description:
Former deputy prime minister Barnaby Joyce discusses the City of Melbourne’s CEO jetting to Cairo despite the City of Melbourne website pushing for citizens to walk and ride to work.

“It’s the typical way of a cult,” Mr Joyce told Sky News host James Macpherson.

“This always happens around these cults; the Australian people are sick of being in this cult.

“Bowen, he lives in this alternate cultish universe.”

episodes.title: ‘Definition of a cult’: Australian Greens co-founder opens up about his expulsion from party
episodes.description: Greens co-founder Drew Hutton meets with Sky News host Chris Kenny to examine the reasoning behind his expulsion from the Australian Greens Party.

episodes.title: Victorian parliamentary hearing into cult groups commences
episodes.description:
Psychologist and cult survivor Maria Esquerra has spoken on the “overdue” parliamentary hearing by the Victorian government into cults and fringe groups. 

“Myself with a lot of other people, mostly who grew up in cults, actually … we got together, and we made a bunch of submissions to Chris Couzens in Victoria in the legislative assembly, and that’s what’s triggered this,” Ms Esquerra told Sky News Australia. 

This hearing states they are not judging people’s religious beliefs, but rather examining groups who harm others emotionally, financially, and physically.
